subject: Reasoning & Mental Ability
note: Practice feed me correct answer nahi hota; jaha ID important-questions se match kiya waha answer bhara hai (answer_source dekho).

questions.question_text: Given that on 9th August 2016 is Saturday. What was the day on 9th August 1616?
questions.solution:
We know that, after every 400 years, the same day occurs.

Thus, if 9th August 2016 is Saturday, before 400 years i.e., on 9th August 1616 has to be Saturday.

questions.question_text: On 17th March, 1997 Monday falls. What day of the week was it on 17th March, 1996?
questions.solution:
The year 1996 is a leap year. So, it has 2 odd days. So, the day on 17th March, 1997 will be 2 days beyond the day on 17th March,1996.

Here 17th March, 1997 is Monday. So, 17th March, 1996 is Saturday.

questions.question_text: On 24th Nov 2007 Thursday falls. What day of the week was it on 10th Nov 2006?
questions.solution:
The year 2006 is an ordinary year. So, it has 1 odd day.

So, the day on 24th Nov, 2007 will be 1 day beyond the day on 24th Nov, 2006.

But, 24th Nov, 2007 is Thursday.

24 - 10 = 14 days.

Therefore, 2 weeks ago it is same day.

Thus, 10th Nov, 2006 is one day before 10th Nov, 2007 i.e. it is Wednesday.

questions.question_text: What will be the day of the week 15th August 2010?
questions.solution:
15th August 2010 = (2009 years + Period 1.1.2010 to 15.8.2010)

Odd days in 1600 years = 0

Odd days in 400 years = 0

9 years = (2 leap years + 7 ordinary years) = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 1) = 11 odd days = 4 odd days.

Jan, Feb, March, April, May, June, July, Aug

(31 + 28 + 31 + 30 + 31 + 30 + 31 + 15) = 227 days

{tex}\therefore{/tex} 227 days = (32 weeks + 3 days) = 3 odd days

Total number of odd days = (0 + 0 + 4 + 3) = 7 = 0 odd days.

The given day is Sunday.

questions.question_text: If 4th December 1999 is Monday then what day is 3rd January 2000?
questions.solution:
Here from 4th December 1999 to 3rd January 2000,

there is a difference of 30 days.

Number of odd days = 30 {tex}\div{/tex} 7 = 2 odd days

Then no. of odd days = 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Tuesday and Wednesday(from Monday).

Hence on 3rd January, it is Wednesday.

questions.question_text: On 1st Jan, 2005 Saturday falls. What day of the week was it on 1st Jan, 2006?
questions.solution:
The year 1985 is an ordinary year. So, it has 1 odd day.

So, the day on 19th June 1985 will be 1 day after the day on 19th June 1984.

But, 19th June 1984 is Monday.

S0, 19th June 1985 is Tuesday.

questions.question_text: The calendar for the year 2013 was the same as the year:
questions.solution:
Given year, 2013 when divided by 4 leaves a remainder of 1.

NOTE: When the remainder is 1, 11 is subtracted from the given year to get the result.

So, 2013 - 11 = 2002

questions.question_text: The calendar of year 1856 is same as which year?
questions.solution:
Given year 1856, when divided by 4 leaves a remainder of 0.

NOTE: When the remainder is 0, 28 is added to the given year to get the result.

So, 1856 + 28 = 1884

questions.question_text: It was Sunday on Jan 1, 2006. What was the day of the week Jan 1, 2010?
questions.solution:
On 31st December 2005, it was Saturday.

Number of odd days from 2006 to 2009 = (1 + 1 + 2 + 1) = 5 days.

On 31st December 2009, it was Thursday.

Thus, on 1st Jan, 2010 it is Friday

questions.question_text: On 8th Feb, 2005 it was Tuesday. What was the day of the week on 8th Feb, 2004?
questions.solution:
The year 2004 is a leap year. It has 2 odd days.

The day on 8th Feb 2004 is 2 days before the day on 8th Feb 2005.

Hence, this day is Sunday.

questions.question_text: The calendar for the year 2018 will be the same for the year:
questions.solution:
How to find the years which have the same Calendars:

Leap year calendar repeats every 28 years.

Here 28 is distributed as 6 + 11 + 11

Rules:



	• If given year is at 1st position after Leap year then next repeated calendar year is Given Year + 6.

	• If given year is at 2nd position after Leap year then next repeated calendar year is Given Year + 11.

	• If given year is at 3rd position after Leap year then next repeated calendar year is Given Year + 11.



Now, the given year is 2018.

We know that 2016 is a Leap year.

2016 - LY; 2017 - 1st; 2018 - 2nd; 2019 - 3rd; 2020 - LY

Here 2018 is at 2nd position after the Leap year.

According to rule (b) the calendar of 2018 is repeated for the year is 2018 + 11 = 2029.

questions.question_text: If Aug 15th, 2012 falls on Thursday then June 11th,2013 falls on which day?
questions.solution:
First, we count the number of odd days for the leftover days in the given period.

Here, the given period is 15.8.2012 to 11.6.2013



	
		
			Aug
			Sept
			Oct
			Nov
			Dec
			Jan
			Feb
			Mar
			Apr
			May
			Jun


		
			16
			30
			31
			30
			31
			31
			28
			31
			30
			31
			11 (days left)


	


2 + 2 + 3 + 2 + 3 + 3 + 0 + 3 + 2 + 3 + 4 (odd days) = 6 odd days

So, given day Thursday + 6 = Wednesday is the required result.

questions.question_text: If Mar 18th,1994 falls on Friday then Feb 25th,1995 falls on which day?
questions.solution:
First, we count the number of odd days for the leftover days in the given period. Here, the given period is 18.3.1994 to 25.2.1995



	
		
			Month
			Mar
			Apr
			May
			Jun
			Jul
			Aug
			Sep
			Oct
			Nov
			Dec
			Jan
			Feb


		
			Days
			13
			30
			31
			30
			31
			31
			30
			31
			30
			31
			31
			25


		
			Odd Days
			6
			2
			3
			2
			3
			3
			2
			3
			2
			3
			3
			4


	


Therefore, No. of Odd Days = 6 + 2 + 3 + 2 + 3 + 3 + 2 + 3 + 2 + 3 + 3 + 4 = 36 = 1 odd day

So, given day Friday + 1 = Saturday is the required result.

questions.question_text: The calendar for the year 1988 is the same as ________ upcoming year.
questions.solution:
We already know that the calendar after a leap year repeats again after 28 years.

Here 1988 is a Leap year, then the same calendar will be in the year = 1988 + 28 = 2016

questions.question_text: The calendar of the year 2024 can be used again in the year:
questions.solution:
Repetition of leap year {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Add +28 to the Given Year.

Repetition of non-leap year

Step 1: Add +11 to the Given Year. If Result is a leap year, Go to step 2.

Step 2:  Add +6 to the Given Year.

Solution:

The given Year is 2024, Which is a leap year.

So, Add +28 to the given year (i.e 2024 + 28) = 2052

Therefore, The calendar of the year 2024 can be used again in the year 2052.

questions.question_text: Which year has 366 days?
questions.solution:
When a century year leaves a remainder 0, when divided by 400 then it is a leap year (366 days).

So, 1200 has 366 days.

questions.question_text: If July 9th, 2013 falls on Sunday then Jan 7th, 2014 falls on which day?
questions.solution:
First, we count the number of odd days for the leftover days in the given period. Here, the given period is 09-07-2013 to 07-01-2014



	
		
			Month
			Jul
			Aug
			Sep
			Oct
			Nov
			Dec
			Jan


		
			Days
			22
			31
			30
			31
			30
			31
			7


		
			Odd Days
			1
			3
			2
			3
			2
			3
			0


	


Therefore, No. of Odd Days =  1 + 3 + 2 + 3 + 2 + 3 + 0 = 14 = 0 odd days

So, given day Sunday + 0 = Saturday is the required result.

questions.question_text: 1.12.91 is the first Sunday. Which is the fourth Tuesday of December 91?
questions.solution:
1/12/91 is the first Sunday of December 91

So, 3/12/91 is the first Tuesday of the month.

Clearly, 10/12/91, 17/12/91, 24/12/91, and 31/12/91 are also Tuesdays.

So, 24/12/91 is the fourth Tuesday.

questions.question_text: If 5th March 2005 is Monday, what was the day of the week on 5th March 2004?
questions.solution:
The year 2004 is a leap year. So, it has 2 odd days.

But, Feb 2004 is not included because we are calculating from March 2004 to March 2005. So it has 1 odd day only.

The day on 5th March 2005 will be 1 day beyond the day on 5th March 2004.

Given that, 5th March 2005 is Monday.

5th March 2004 is Sunday (1 day before to 5th March 2005).

questions.question_text: What was the day on 2nd Jan 1901?
questions.solution:
2nd Jan 1901 means

(1900 years and 2 days)

Now, 1600 years have 0 odd day

300 years have 1 odd day

2 days have 2 odd day

Total no. of odd days = 0 + 1 + 2 = 3 days

Hence, the day on 2nd Jan 1901 was Wednesday.

questions.question_text: What was the day on 16th June,1993?
questions.solution:
16 June, 1993 = (1992 years + Period from 1.1.1993 to 16.6.1993)

Odd days in 1600 years = 0

Odd days in 300 years = 1

92 years = (69 ordinary years + 23 leap year) = (69 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 + 23 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) = 3 odd days

Jan, Feb, March, April, May, June

(31 + 28 + 31 + 30 + 31 + 16) = 167 days

167 days = (23 weeks + 6 days) = 6 odd days.

Total number of odd days = (0 + 1 + 3 + 6) = 3 odd days

Given day is Wednesday.

questions.question_text: Which calendar was used again in the year 1856?
questions.solution:
Given year 1856, when divided by 4 leaves a remainder of 0.

NOTE: When the remainder is 0, 28 is subtracted to the given year to get the result.

So, 1856 - 28 = 1828

questions.question_text: What will be the day on 18th Feb, 2016?
questions.solution:
18 Feb, 2016 = (2015 years + Period from 1.1.2016 to 18.2.2016)

Odd days in 1600 years = 0

Odd days in 400 years = 0

15 years = (12 ordinary years + 3 leap year) = (12 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 + 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 2)= 4 odd days

Jan Feb

(31 + 18) = 49 days

49 days = (7 weeks + 0 day) = 0 odd day

Total number of odd days = (0 + 0 + 4 + 0) = 4 odd day

Given day is Thursday

questions.question_text: If 6th March, 2005 is Monday, what was the day of the week on 6th March, 2004?
questions.solution:
The year 2004 is a leap year. So, it has 2 odd days.

But, Feb 2004 not included because we are calculating from March 2004 to March 2005.

So it has 1 odd day only.

The day on 6th March, 2005 will be 1 day beyond the day on 6th March, 2004.

Given that, 6th March, 2005 is Monday.

6th March, 2004 is Sunday (1 day before to 6th March, 2005).

questions.question_text: On 8th Dec, 2007 Saturday falls. What day of the week was it on 8th Dec, 2006?
questions.solution:
The year 2006 is an ordinary year. So, it has 1 odd day.

So, the day on 8th Dec, 2007 will be 1 day beyond the day on 8th Dec, 2006.

But, 8th Dec, 2007 is Saturday

So, 8th Dec, 2006 is Friday.

questions.question_text: January 1, 2007 was Monday. What day of the week lies on Jan 1, 2008?
questions.solution:
The year 2007 is an ordinary year. So, it has 1 odd day.

1st day of the year 2007 was Monday

1st day of the year 2008 will be 1 day beyond Monday

Hence, it will be Tuesday.

questions.question_text: The calendar for the year 2007 will be the same for the year:
questions.solution:
Count the number of odd days from the year 2007 onwards to get the sum equal to 0 odd days.



	
		
			Year
			2007
			2008
			2009
			2010
			2011
			2012
			2013
			2014
			2015
			2016
			2017


		
			Odd day
			1
			2
			1
			1
			1
			2
			1
			1
			1
			2
			1


	


Sum = 14 odd days = 0 odd days

{tex}\therefore{/tex} The calendar for the year 2018 will be the same for the year 2007.

questions.question_text: The calendar of the year 1897 can be used again in the year:
questions.solution:
Repetition of leap year

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Add +28 to the Given Year.

Repetition of a non-leap year

Step 1: Add +11 to the Given Year. If Result is a leap year, Go to step 2.

Step 2: Add +6 to the Given Year.

Solution:

The given Year is 1897, Which is a non-leap year.

Step 1: Add +11 to the given year (i.e 1897 + 11) = 1908, Which is a leap year.

Step 2: Add +6 to the given year (i.e 1897 + 6) = 1903

Therefore, The calendar of the year 1897 can be used again in the year 1903

questions.question_text: What was the day of the week on 16th August 1947?
questions.solution:
15th August, 1947 = (1946 years + Period from 1st Jan, 1947 to 15th)

Counting of odd days:

1600 years have 0 odd day. 300 years have 1 odd day.

47 years = (11 leap years + 36 ordinary years) = [(11 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) + (36 {tex}\times{/tex} 1) ]odd days = 58 odd days = 2 odd days

Jan, Feb, Mar, Apr, May, Jun, Jul, Aug.

31 + 28 + 31 + 30 + 31 + 30 + 31 + 15 = 227 days = (32 weeks + 3 days) = 3

Total number of odd days = (0 + 1 + 2 + 3) odd days = 6 odd days

Hence, the required day was 'Saturday'.

questions.question_text: The second & fourth Saturdays and every Sunday is a holiday. How many working days will be there in a month of 31 days beginning on a Friday?
questions.solution:
Given that the month begins on a Friday and has 31 days

Sundays = 3rd, 10th, 17th, 24th, 31st

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Total Sundays = 5

Every second & fourth Saturday is a holiday.

2nd & 4th Saturday in every month = 2

Total days in the month = 31

Total working days = 31 - (5 + 2) = 24 days.

questions.question_text: What was the day of the week on 28th May 2006?
questions.solution:
28 May, 2006 = (2005 years + Period from 1.1.2006 to 28.5.2006)

Odd days in 1600 years = 0

Odd days in 400 years = 0

5 years = (4 ordinary years + 1 leap years) = (4 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 + 1 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) = 6 odd days

Jan. Feb. March. April. May (31 + 28 + 31 + 30 + 28) = 148 days

{tex}\therefore{/tex} 148 days = 21 weeks + 1 day = 1 odd day

Total number of odd days = (0 + 0 + 6 + 1) = 7 = 0 odd day

Given day is Sunday.

questions.question_text: Oct 1st, 1994 falls on which day?
questions.solution:
1 Oct, 1994 = (1993 years + Period from 1.1.1993 to 1.10.1993)

Odd days in 1600 years = 0

Odd days in 300 years = 1

93 years = (70 ordinary years + 23 leap year) = (70 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 + 23 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) = 4 odd days

Jan, Feb, March, April, May, June, July, Aug, sept, Oct

(31 + 28 + 31 + 30 + 31 + 30 + 31 + 31 + 30 + 1 ) = 274 days

274 days = (39 weeks + 1 day) = 1 odd day.

Total number of odd days = (0 + 1 + 4 + 1) = 6 odd days.

The given day is Saturday.

questions.question_text: What was the day on 31st Dec, 2011?
questions.solution:
31st Dec, 2011 = (2010 years + Period from 1.1.2011 to 31.12.2011)

Odd days in 2000 years = 0

10 years has 2 leap years + 8 ordinary years.

Number of odd days in 10 years ( 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + 8) = 5 odd days.

31st,Dec {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} complete year of 2011 (non-leap year) = 1 odd day.

Total number of odd days = (0 + 5 + 1) = 6.

The given day is saturday.

questions.question_text: What was the day on 9th May 2001?
questions.solution:
9th May, 2001 = (2000 years + Period from 1.1.2001 to 9.5.2001)

Odd days in 1600 years = 0

Odd days in 400 years = 0

Jan. Feb. March April May

(31 + 28 + 31 + 30 + 9) = 129 days

129 days = (18 weeks + 3 day) = 3 odd day

Total number of odd days = (0 + 0 + 3 ) = 3 odd day

Given day is Wednesday.

questions.question_text: April 19th, 1986 falls on which day?
questions.solution:
19th April, 1986 = (1985 years + Period from 1.1.1986 to 19.4.1986)

Odd days in 1600 years = 0

Odd days in 300 years = 1

85 years has 21 leap years + 64 ordinary years.

Number of odd days in 85 years =( 21 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + 64) = 1 odd day.

Jan, Feb, March, April

(31 + 28 + 31 + 19) = 109 days

109 days = 15 weeks + 4 days = 4 odd day

Total number of odd days = (1 + 0 + 1 + 4) = 6

Given day is Saturday.

questions.question_text: What was the day on 25th Jan, 1981?
questions.solution:
25 Jan, 1981 = (1980 years + Period from 1.1.1981 to 25.1.1981)

Odd days in 1600 years = 0

Odd days in 300 years = 1

80 years = (60 ordinary years + 20 leap year) = (60 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 + 20 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) = 2 odd days

Jan = 25 days = (3 weeks + 4 days) = 4 odd days

Total number of odd days = (0 + 1 + 2 + 4) = 7= 0 odd day

The given day is Sunday.

questions.question_text: What was the day of the week on 17th June, 1998?
questions.solution:
17th June, 1998 = (1997 years + Period from 1.1.1998 to 17.6.1998)

Odd days in 1600 years = 0

Odd days in 300 years = (5 {tex}\times{/tex} 3) 1

97 years has 24 leap years + 73 ordinary years.

Number of odd days in 97 years (24 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + 73) = 121 = 2 odd days.

Jan, Feb, March, April, May, June

(31 + 28 + 31 + 30 + 31 + 17) = 168 days

168 days = 24 weeks = 0 odd day

Total number of odd days = (0 + 1 + 2 + 0) = 3.

Given day is Wednesday

questions.question_text: How many leap years do 100 years have?
questions.solution:
The given year is divided by 4, and the quotient gives the number of leap years.

Here, 100 {tex}\div{/tex} 4 = 25.

But, as 100 is not a leap year {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 25 - 1= 24 leap years.

questions.question_text: What is 90 days from today? (Hints: Today is 20th January 2017, Sunday)
questions.solution:
Given Today is 20th January 2017, Sunday

In january, we have 31 days

February - 28 days (Non leap year)

March - 31 days

April - 30 days

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Remaining days {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 31 - 20 = 11 in Jan

+ 28 in Feb + 31 in Mar = 11 + 28 + 31 = 70 days

More 20 days to complete 90 days {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} upto 20th April

Therefore, after 90 days from today i.e, 20th Jan 2017 is 20th Apr 2017.

Now, the day of the week will be

90/7 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Remainder '6'

As the day starts with '0' on Sunday

6 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Saturday.

The required day is 20th April, Saturday.

questions.question_text: How many leap years do 300 years have?
questions.solution:
The given year is divided by 4, and the quotient gives the number of leap years.

Here, 300 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 75.

But, as 100, 200 and 300 are not in leap years {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 75 - 3 = 72 leap years.

questions.question_text: If today is Saturday, what will be the day 350 days from now?
questions.solution:
350 days = (350 {tex}\div{/tex} 7 = 50 weeks) i.e no odd days,

So it will be a Saturday.

questions.question_text: If Feb 12th, 1986 falls on Wednesday then Jan 1st, 1987 falls on which day?
questions.solution:
First, we count the number of odd days for the leftover days in the given period.

Here, the given period is 12.2.1986 to 1.1.1987



	
		
			Feb
			Mar
			Apr
			May
			June
			July
			Aug
			Sept
			Oct
			Nov
			Dec
			Jan


		
			16
			31
			30
			31
			30
			31
			31
			30
			31
			30
			31
			1 (day left)


	


2 + 3 + 2 + 3 + 2 + 3 + 3 + 2 + 3 + 2 + 3 + 1 (odd days) = 1 odd day

So, given day Wednesday + 1 = Thursday is the required result.
